libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Set `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED` for JVM test execution and native-image build arguments when tests exercise Guice/CGLIB enhancer or fast-class generation, because the bundled relocated CGLIB reflectively accesses `ClassLoader#defineClass`. Do not add external Guava or CGLIB to recover old symbols; in the 2.9.1 main artifact the internal relocated classes are named with a leading `$` such as `com.google.inject.internal.util.$MapMaker`, `$FinalizableReferenceQueue`, `com.google.inject.internal.cglib.core.$Signature`, and `com.google.inject.internal.cglib.proxy.$MethodProxy`, so tests should use public Guice APIs or those actual 2.9.1 class names.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8858 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.sonatype.sisu:sisu-guice:2.9.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The failing javac symbols come from version-sensitive internal classes, not missing dependencies; tests that target internals must be adjusted for the 2.9.1 jar layout.
CGLIB bytecode generation and reflective class definition are sensitive under modern JDKs and native image, so local Gradle and Native Image verification remain authoritative.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker image is needed; meaningful CGLIB/bytecode-generation coverage needs the existing module-open JVM/native setup, and 2.9.1 uses jarjar `$`-prefixed internal class names.
